question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user send rapid repeated subscribeTopic() calls with valid topicIds, causing entityRepository.findById() to be called for each subscription and saturating the entity cache lookup path, creating a cache stampede that degrades performance on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions with endTime set to a value just 1 nanosecond after startTime, causing the subscription to remain open for the full endTimeInterval (30s) before isComplete() returns true, and by opening many such subscriptions in rapid succession, maintain a constant pool of resource-consuming subscriptions that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unthrottled path (throttled=false) in topicMessageRetriever.retrieve(gapFilter, false) triggered by the safety check, which uses a higher maxPageSize and faster pollingFrequency, to generate more aggressive DB queries per subscriber than the throttled path, amplifying DB load and crashing 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions to many different topicIds simultaneously, causing the Redis listener to create a separate channel subscription per topic (topic.{id}), exhausting Redis connection resources and causing the RedisTopicListener to fail, which propagates errors to all subscribers and crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the isNext() check to fail by exploiting a TOCTOU race between getLast() in isNext() and last.set() in onNext(), where two concurrent reactive threads deliver messages simultaneously, causing an IllegalStateException to be thrown and terminating the subscription with an error that is logged at ERROR level, flooding logs and degrading I/O on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions with a limit of 1 and startTime=0, causing each subscription to retrieve one historical message and complete, but by opening millions of such short-lived subscriptions in rapid succession, generate a continuous stream of DB queries and reactive pipeline setup/teardown overhead that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that each call to subscribeTopic() creates a new TopicContext with a Stopwatch.createStarted(), AtomicLong, and AtomicReference, and by opening millions of subscriptions, cause excessive GC pressure from short-lived objects that triggers GC pauses long enough to crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription where incomingMessages() builds a newFilter via filter.toBuilder().limit(limit).startTime(startTime).build() with limit=0 (when hasLimit() is false), causing topicListener.listen(newFilter) to receive a filter with limit=0 that bypasses hasLimit() checks downstream and streams unlimited messages, amplifying memory usage on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions to a topic that has no messages, causing the safetyCheck to fire after 1 second and call missingMessages(topicContext, null) which calls topicMessageRetriever.retrieve(gapFilter, false), and since the unthrottled retriever has maxPolls=12 by default, generate 12 DB queries per subscriber per safety check cycle, multiplied across thousands of subscribers to crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: pastEndTime()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value that causes isComplete() to always return false (e.g., endTime = Long.MAX_VALUE), causing pastEndTime() to run RepeatSpec.create(r -> !topicContext.isComplete(), Long.MAX_VALUE) with Long.MAX_VALUE repetitions, creating a near-infinite timer loop per subscription that accumulates scheduler tasks and crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions with a startTime that is the maximum valid nanosecond value (just below DomainUtils.now()), causing the historical retriever to return immediately with no results but the live listener to remain open indefinitely, and by accumulating thousands of such live-only subscriptions, exhaust listener buffer capacity (maxBufferSize=16384) and trigger BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that missingMessages() calls topicMessageRetriever.retrieve(newFilter, false) with an unthrottled retriever that has no timeout enforcement in the gap-fill path, causing a slow DB query to hold a reactive thread indefinitely and starve other subscriptions on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions with endTime set to a value in the past relative to startTime, which would fail isValidEndTime() validation, but if validation is bypassed or misconfigured, cause isComplete() to return true immediately while still consuming resources during the subscription setup phase, and by repeating this at high rate, degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: TopicContext.rate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an integer overflow in rate() by maintaining a subscription that processes more than Integer.MAX_VALUE / 1000 messages per millisecond, causing (int)(1000.0 * count.get() / elapsed) to overflow and produce a negative rate value that is logged, potentially corrupting monitoring metrics and masking the attack on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions to a topic with a very high message rate, causing the filter lambda in flux.filter(t -> { TopicMessage last = topicContext.getLast(); return last == null || t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber(); }) to be evaluated for every message with a getLast() call, creating high-frequency AtomicReference reads that cause cache-line contention and degrade throughput on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions with a topicId that resolves to a valid topic but has no messages, causing the live subscription to remain open indefinitely via Flux.never() from pastEndTime() when endTime is null, and by accumulating thousands of such idle subscriptions, exhaust file descriptor limits and crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions where the live listener delivers messages faster than the concatMap(t -> missingMessages(topicContext, t)) can process them, causing unbounded backpressure buildup in the reactive pipeline that eventually triggers an OutOfMemoryError and crashes 30%+ of nodes?
